data.body:
PASS 1 review (E4 — assumption/premise verification) for Cluster 1. Forced a Read/Grep probe for EVERY codebase assertion in the plan; training knowledge not used. All material premises verified TRUE — no unverifiable/false-premise gaps, so no E4 findings.

AFFIRMATIONS (each verified against the named implementation file):
- "src/rebar/_store/canonical.py is the single home" + "today's positional signature canonical_str(event) is unchanged": VERIFIED — canonical.py:39-47 defines canonical_str(event)/canonical_bytes(event) positionally with ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":") and no trailing newline; content_hash is genuinely absent today, consistent with the plan's "extend". Additive keyword-only params are therefore backward-compatible.
- "the six existing importers call positionally → untouched/byte-identical": VERIFIED — grep confirms exactly six src importers, all positional: event_append.py:114, _commands/txn.py:220/394/415, delete.py:114/205, compact.py:139, inbound_translate.py:239, batch_dispatch.py:238. The three test importers (test_canonical.py:22, test_e3_txn_bytes.py:19, test_canonical_event_bytes.py:22) also call positionally → unaffected.
- "signing.py _canonical_payload (ensure_ascii=False) → seam default ascii_only=False, byte-identical": VERIFIED — signing.py:187-199 uses sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":"), ensure_ascii=False.
- "schema.py canonical_json/content_hash (ensure_ascii=False) → seam defaults; keep public names as re-exports; preserve __all__": VERIFIED — schema.py:390-403 (ensure_ascii=False; content_hash = sha256 of canonical_json). llm/workflow/__init__.py imports both (:56-57) and lists both in __all__ (:85-86).
- "conflict_resolver._hash_value: ascii_only=True REQUIRED + default=str load-bearing": VERIFIED and the reasoning is factually grounded — conflict_resolver.py:215-219 is json.dumps(value, sort_keys=True, default=str, separators=(",",":")) with NO ensure_ascii= argument, so it uses the stdlib default ensure_ascii=True. The plan's mandate to pass ascii_only=True (to reproduce \uXXXX escaping) AND preserve default=str is correct; value_hash feeds record()/is_echo (:253-273), so the byte-preservation rationale is sound.
- "mutation.serialize_manifest: PRESERVE ensure_ascii=True via ascii_only=True": VERIFIED — mutation.py:106 uses explicit ensure_ascii=True.
- Feasibility of the cross-package seam import (a plausible hidden hazard): VERIFIED reachable — conflict_resolver.py/mutation.py are siblings of inbound_translate.py:218 and batch_dispatch.py:211, which already do `from rebar._store.canonical import canonical_str` from inside the rebar_reconciler package. No new namespace/boundary hazard.

Note (NOT an E4 gap): the prior-cycle conflict_resolver byte-divergence concern is RESOLVED in this plan revision — the "What" bullet and AC already specify `ascii_only=True, default=str` with the full stdlib-default reasoning. No premise in the current text is contradicted by source. Clean for E4.
